results.description: Proves the current torch_patch summary tracks explicit fake-CUDA storages but does not account for most op-produced activation tensors.
results.excerpt:
after_xy: 8.0 MB
after_add: 8.0 MB
after_clone: 8.0 MB
after_zeros_like: 8.0 MB
peak: 8.0 MB
alloc_calls: 2
results.note: This is an expected limitation probe, not a bug regression. It documents that op-produced tensors like `x + y` are not yet tracked.
results.stderr:
/Users/l1ght/repos/FakeGPU/fakegpu/_runtime.py:97: UserWarning: fakegpu.torch_patch: CUDA operations are transparently redirected to CPU. Tensor.device will report 'cpu'. Computations are real but run on the CPU backend.
  return patch(num_devices=num_devices, device_name=device_name)

results.title: Summary Scope Probe (Elementwise + Clone)

results.description: Shows that Report Summary peak memory scales with parameter count for weight-only model loading.
results.excerpt:
params: 520.22M
fp32_weight_bytes: 1.94 GiB
memory_allocated: 1.94 GiB
memory_peak: 1.94 GiB
alloc_calls: 255
results.note: Peak memory matches fp32 parameter bytes, which is the correct behavior for the current weight-only tracker.

results.title: Load-Only Scaling (520M MoE)

results.description: Confirms the same weight-tracking behavior at roughly 1B parameters.
results.excerpt:
params: 1001.61M
fp32_weight_bytes: 3.73 GiB
memory_allocated: 3.73 GiB
memory_peak: 3.73 GiB
alloc_calls: 507
results.note: The 1B-class model still reports weight-size peak memory rather than full optimizer+activation training memory.
